1_blaA_clinical_enrichment.finding: blaA strongly enriched in clinical and food chain niches
1_blaA_clinical_enrichment.interpretation: blaA is under strong positive selection in human clinical and food chain contexts in Europe and North America, with a dramatic temporal increase (OR=49.82 in 2020+). Markedly depleted in wildlife (rodent, marmot, fish) and Central Asia suggesting anthropogenic selection pressure from beta-lactam use in human medicine and food animal production.

2_blaYRC_animal_enrichment.finding: blaYRC uniquely enriched in animal niche and fish hosts
2_blaYRC_animal_enrichment.interpretation: blaYRC shows extreme enrichment in fish hosts (OR=29,220), consistent with its known role in Y. ruckeri (enteric red mouth disease in salmonids). This represents a clear animal-specific AMR lineage with minimal human spillover risk.

3_south_america_integron_cluster.finding: South America: unique integron-associated AMR cluster
3_south_america_integron_cluster.interpretation: A distinct multi-drug resistance gene cluster is uniquely enriched in South America (g+t+: 9-12 genomes), including streptothricin (sat2), trimethoprim (dfrA1), chloramphenicol phenicol (floR), and aminoglycosides (aadA1). This co-occurrence pattern suggests a South American-specific integron or plasmid carrying multiple resistance determinants — a candidate for MOBsuite plasmid characterization.

4_europe_aminoglycoside_cluster.interpretation: European isolates show enrichment for class 1 integron-associated genes (aadA12, qacEdelta1, sul1), consistent with the known prevalence of IncI and IncF plasmids in European Y. enterocolitica clinical isolates. These are priority targets for plasmid reconstruction with MOBsuite.

5_environmental_AMR_richness.finding: Environmental niche harbors highest AMR gene diversity
5_environmental_AMR_richness.interpretation: Despite being the smallest niche group (n=91 genomes), Environmental isolates harbor all 69 unique AMR genes — far exceeding the 8 genes in clinical (H) and animal (A) niches. This supports the environmental resistome as a reservoir for AMR genes not yet detected in clinical surveillance. The novel circumpolar Yersinia sp. nov. belongs to this niche — its AMR profile is part of this cryptic environmental resistome undetectable by current marker-based surveillance.

6_temporal_AMR_increase.finding: Dramatic temporal increase in blaA/vat(F) post-2020
6_temporal_AMR_increase.interpretation: The 2020+ era shows 49-fold enrichment for blaA and 39-fold for vat(F) compared to baseline. This likely reflects both increased sequencing of clinical isolates post-COVID and genuine AMR selection pressure. Requires temporal stratification analysis to disentangle sequencing bias from true AMR emergence.

paper_implications.paper1_validation: Environmental niche harbors 69/69 AMR genes vs 8 in clinical — surveillance-neglected environmental species carry the full resistome diversity. The novel circumpolar clade in niche_E is part of this cryptic mobilome.
paper_implications.paper2_panmob: South American integron cluster and European aminoglycoside cluster are prime targets for MOBsuite/Platon plasmid characterization — PanMob toolkit validation cases.
priority_targets: blaYRC in Y. ruckeri fish isolates
